prompt:
Build a Workforce Training & Operations Hub that supports secure multi-role access, on-site scheduling, skills assessment, and controlled discounting for a US-based organization running entirely on an internal network. The React web UI serves distinct workspaces for trainees (students/participants), supervisors (teachers/coaches), guardians (for youth programs), employers (partner companies), and administrators (career center and college admins). Users sign in with a locally verified username and password and land on a role-based dashboard that only shows authorized menus and actions. Trainees can browse available training sessions and book time slots generated by staff/resource calendars, with clear availability indicators, buffer times (default 10 minutes), and automatic release of an unconfirmed booking after 15 minutes. Reschedule and cancel actions show policy prompts (for example, cancellations within 12 hours are blocked unless an admin overrides with a reason). Supervisors run assessments from configurable templates (time-based, rep-based, or combined), and trainees see real-time progress toward rank levels; certificates can be generated as printable PDFs with a visible verification code and status. Guardians can link up to five child accounts, manage approved devices per child, and trigger remote session logout if a device is lost. Admins use a moderation console to review and score user-generated content such as session notes, portfolio evidence, and instructor feedback, with bulk approve/reject, reason capture, and a clear audit trail. A voucher center enables admins to issue coupons (for example, $25 off a $150+ certification fee) with claim limits, expiration dates, and applicability rules displayed at redemption time.

On the system side, the backend uses Symfony to expose REST-style APIs consumed by the React frontend over the local LAN, enforcing RBAC at both route and action levels and logging every sensitive operation with timestamp, actor, and before/after values for traceability. MySQL persists core entities such as accounts, role assignments, guardian-child links, sessions, bookings, assessments, ranks, vouchers, and moderation decisions; passwords are stored using a modern salted hash, and sensitive profile identifiers (real-name/credential verification artifacts) are encrypted at rest with key material stored locally. The scheduling engine validates conflicts at commit time, applies recurrence rules and ad-hoc leave, and maintains consistent slot states with transactional locking; timeouts revert reserved slots automatically without requiring external job services. The voucher state machine enforces claim, lock, redeem, and void transitions with idempotent redemption keys and row-level concurrency controls to prevent over-issuance during simultaneous checkouts. Content moderation combines basic automated checks (file type/size limits, checksum-based duplicate detection, prohibited-word matching) with manual review queues and quality scoring, without any cloud scanning. For storage and indexing, operational data remains in MySQL while larger historical artifacts (certificate PDFs, attachments, snapshots) are stored on local disk with hot/cold tiering rules (for example, move items older than 180 days to a cold directory) and scheduled snapshot exports for reporting; partitioning by month for high-volume logs supports fast querying while preserving offline reliability.

/eagle-initial
